data.title: 3 High-Yield Dividend Aristocrat Stocks to Watch — June 12, 2026
data.description:
Amcor (AMCR), Realty Income (O), and Hormel Foods (HRL) are the three high-yield dividend aristocrat stocks with support from top Wall Street analysts.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The TipRanks Dividend Aristocrat tool shows that the least of the trio offers a dividend yield of 5.21%.

Dividend aristocrats are companies that belong to the benchmark S&P 500 index (SPX) and that have consistently raised their dividend payments every year for a minimum of 25 years. These stocks offer investors steady income and market growth opportunity.

Why These 3 Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Stand Out

According to Wall Street analysts, the three stocks listed below are worth buying. The least of the trio has a dividend payout ratio of 68.36%, meaning that the company recently distributed more than half of its net earnings to shareholders as dividends. Furthermore, each company currently has a Buy rating based on consensus from top analysts or broader Wall Street agreement, with significant upside potential.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today's top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. Amcor (AMCR) — Amcor is a Swiss consumer packaging multinational that develops and supplies flexible packaging, including wraps, bags, and pouches. It also manufactures rigid packaging products such as plastic bottles, jars and containers. The Zurich-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 68.36%. It paid 65 cents a share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 6.48%. AMCR stock currently carries a Moderate Buy consensus rating from six Wall Street analysts. This comes with an average price target of $46.80, implying more than 15% upside.
  2. Realty Income (O) — Realty Income is popularly known as "the monthly dividend company" due to its goal of generating monthly returns for investors from its investment in commercial properties. The California-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 530.65%. O paid 27 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 5.23%. Its shares currently carry a Moderate Buy consensus rating from 13 analysts on Wall Street. This comes with an average price target of $68.63 that suggests more than 9% upside.
  3. Hormel Foods (HRL) — Hormel is a U.S.-based global food processing company known for brands such as Skippy peanut butter, Planters peanuts, and SPAM luncheon meat. The Minnesota-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 165.25%. HRL paid 29 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 4.77%. Its shares currently carry a Moderate Buy consensus rating from six Wall Street analysts. This comes with an average price target of $26 that suggests about 5% upside.

More on Dividend Aristocrats

Some observers also treat recent spin-offs as dividend aristocrats if their parent companies meet the required track record, effectively granting the offshoot the parent's dividend history.

Interested in More Dividend Aristocrat Stocks?

To find more stocks like these, take a look at TipRanks' Dividend Aristocrats tool.

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Surpasses Tesla to Become Sixth Most Valuable U.S. Company after IPO
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) surpassed Tesla (TSLA) as the most valuable company in Elon Musk's portfolio following its historic initial public offering (IPO) on Friday. In fact, the rocket manufacturer became the sixth most valuable U.S.-listed company. After opening at $150 a share, the stock closed over 19% higher on the first day of trading, pushing SpaceX's market cap above $2 trillion, higher than Tesla's $1.3 trillion.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

200% short exposure to AMZN with AMZO

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

Tesla remains a major force in electric vehicles and energy storage, but its valuation has cooled as competition rises and growth slows. SpaceX, meanwhile, is seen as early in its commercial expansion, with Starlink, deep‑space missions, and government contracts all driving optimism.

Only Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), and Nvidia (NVDA) sit above it, giants that took decades to reach similar heights. For SpaceX to join that group on day one reflects both the scale of its business and the market's belief that the next decade of growth in Musk's portfolio may come from space, not cars.

SpaceX Surge Lifts Musk Into the Trillionaire Club

SpaceX's blockbuster debut did more than set new records; it also pushed Elon Musk to a whole new level of wealth. Following the IPO, Musk's stake in SPCX is now worth far more than his holdings in Tesla, making SpaceX the primary driver of his net worth.

Together with his stakes in Tesla, X, and his other ventures, SpaceX's surge made Musk the world's first trillionaire.

What makes this even more striking is how early SpaceX is in its growth. Investors are placing big bets on the long‑term potential of Starlink and deep‑space missions, areas where SpaceX is still building. If the company keeps growing at its current pace, Musk's wealth could climb even higher.

Is SPCX Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on SPCX stock based on one Buy and one Sell assigned in the past three months. Further, the average SpaceX price target of $152.50 per share implies 11.68% downside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: 3 'Strong Buy' Data Center Stocks with 30%+ Upside — June 12, 2026
data.description:
Applied Digital (APLD), GE Vernova (GEV), and Dycom (DY) are the three data center-related companies that have more than 30% upside on Wall Street. These stocks are also rated a Strong Buy by analysts, according to TipRanks' Compare Top Data Center Stocks tool.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Double your GEV exposure with Tradr's GEVX

The three businesses provide critical infrastructure that helps to keep the AI innovation race alive.

Applied Digital (APLD)

Applied Digital is a Texas-based data center developer that is attracting attention on Wall Street. The company started out as a Bitcoin and Ethereum miner but has put its focus on developing capacity for AI workloads. This week, the AI cloud infrastructure company secured its third 15-year lease agreement with the same U.S.-based hyperscaler.

The company builds facilities it calls Applied AI Factories and has now contracted out 1.4 gigawatts of critical IT load across its five data center campuses, with $86 billion in total contracted lease revenue if all options are exercised.

Applied Digital's shares have jumped over 74% year-to-date. Yet, analysts see about 62% upside ahead based on an average price target of $69.11.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Here's Why Top B. Riley Analyst Raised Marvell (MRVL) Stock Price Target by More than 40%
data.description:
On Thursday, Marvell Technology (MRVL) named Adobe's (ADBE) finance head, Dan Durn, as its new CFO, succeeding Willem Meintjes, who is stepping down after more than three years in the role. The CFO change comes as the company benefits from demand for its custom AI chips and networking products. B. Riley analyst Craig Ellis reiterated a Buy rating on MRVL stock and raised his price target by nearly 44% to $345 from $240. The analyst views the "strong" CFO appointment as another favorable development following CEO Matthew Murphy's Computex keynote, where he was joined by Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang, and the news of MRVL's inclusion in the S&P 500 Index (SPX).

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

200% short exposure to NVDA with NVDS

B. Riley Analyst Is Bullish on MRVL Stock

Ellis believes that the joint CEO appearance at the Computex event indicates that Nvidia's partnership with Marvell has expanded quickly. Notably, the two companies are collaborating on custom AI chips, NVLink Fusion, as well as Celestial and in-house optics. Moreover, the 5-star analyst noted that Huang's statement that Marvell is "the next $1 trillion company" bodes well for its expanded SAM (serviceable addressable market) revenue conversion.

Secondly, Ellis views Marvell's S&P 500 inclusion on June 22 favorably and expects it to expand MRVL's ownership base, both directly and indirectly.

Finally, Ellis reacted positively to Durn's appointment as Marvell's new CFO. The analyst highlighted that Durn's appointment as CFO follows his two years as a director on the company's board. He also noted Durn's 30-plus years of experience, including CFO roles at Adobe (ADBE), Applied Materials (AMAT), NXP (NXPI), Freescale Semiconductor (FSL), and GlobalFoundries (GFS). Ellis added that his interaction with Durn at a prior role at Applied Materials reflected that he is a "strong financial leader with a clear strategic focus and strong operational grasp," which the analyst believes could drive MRVL stock higher over the long term.

Is MRVL Stock a Strong Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Marvell Technology stock based on 24 Buys and four Holds. The average MRVL stock price target of $252 indicates 10% downside risk. MRVL stock has rallied 229% year-to-date.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: ROKU Stock Shoots Up After Reports of Sale Talks With a Major Media Company
data.description:
Roku (ROKU) shares soared 21% on Friday after a Bloomberg report that the streaming company is exploring a potential sale and has held discussions with at least one major U.S. media company. The talks are still early, and no final decisions have been made. Also, there is no guarantee the discussions will lead to a deal.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Importantly, any deal would need the support of Roku founder and CEO Anthony Wood, who owns 11.6% of the company and controls the majority of voting power through his Class B shares. This gives him major influence over any potential sale.

Adding to the positive momentum, Evercore ISI analyst Robert Coolbrith raised his Roku price target to $185 from $160 and kept a Buy rating. He said Roku is set up for strong growth and believes the company's new home screen will drive better ad results and open more ways to make money from its large user base.

Why a Buyer Might Be Interested in Roku

Roku helped shape the streaming era by turning any TV into a smart TV through its devices and operating system. The company now sells its own TVs and projectors and runs The Roku Channel, its free, ad-supported streaming service.

Roku's reach is huge. The company said in April that its devices are used by more than half of all U.S. broadband homes. That scale has made Roku a key entry point for streaming services and for advertisers trying to reach cord‑cutters. It must be noted that the company makes the bulk of its money from the platform business, which includes digital advertising, content distribution, and subscription revenue sharing.

For a media company, Roku offers a huge and active streaming audience, a strong ad platform, and a growing hardware and OS ecosystem. These factors could appeal to a media company that wants to grow its streaming reach or have more control over how its shows get to viewers.

Is ROKU Stock a Good Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on ROKU stock based on 20 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average Roku price target of $153.15 per share implies 6.61% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Jump as U.S.-Iran Near Deal; SpaceX Clinches Historic Debut
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Friday after both the U.S. and Iran touted progress toward a memorandum of understanding (MOU).

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • A senior U.S. official said the odds of an MOU being signed have increased to between 80% and 85%. "We feel very good about the agreement," said the official. "We're not at the finish line but we're very close to it."
  • That came after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi posted on X that the chances of a MOU between the two sides have "never been closer." President Trump screenshotted Araghchi's statement and posted it on his Truth Social account.
  • The preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment for June was 48.9, beating the expectation of 46. The index fell to an all-time low of 44.8 in May.
  • Year-ahead inflation expectations fell to 4.6% from 4.8%. They were 3.4% before the war in late February. Long-term expectations also declined to 3.4% from 3.9%.
  • SpaceX (SPCX) opened at $150 per share, up from its initial IPO price of $135. The spaceflight company offered a total of 555.6 million shares at a $1.77 trillion valuation.
  • Nasdaq (NDAQ) announced that Astera Labs (ALAB), CoreWeave (CRWV), Neibus (NBIS), Rocket Lab (RKLB), and Teradyne (TER) would be added to the Nasdaq 100.
  • Charter Communications (CHTR), Cognizant Technology (CTSH), Insmed Incorporated (INSM), Verisk Analytics (VRSK), and Zscaler (ZS) will be removed from the Nasdaq 100.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: SpaceX Stock (SPCX) Lands with a Bang after Historic IPO — What Lies Ahead?
data.description:
Elon Musk's rocket company SpaceX's (SPCX) initial public offering (IPO) is finally here and it made a huge splash, with SPCX soaring by over 30% within minutes of its public debut. However, the hardest part lies ahead as SpaceX has to prove doubters wrong by pushing Starship to full commercial use and showing that investors can bank on its bet on futuristic fields.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

This includes plans tied to areas such as powering data centers in space using the sun and engaging in Mars missions.

Key highlights from SpaceX's IPO include:

  • A historic $75 billion raise
  • A starting price of $150 above the initial IPO price of $135
  • A $1.77 trillion valuation that makes the company the eighth largest in the world

What Risks do Investors Face with SpaceX?

  • AI Gamble: SpaceX's pre-IPO filings revealed the cost of Musk's massive push into artificial intelligence. The rocket company reported operating loss of $1.94 billion during its first-quarter fiscal 2026 that ended on March 31. xAI, which is the brain behind the Grok chatbot, lost $2.47 billion during that quarter that gobbling 76% of SpaceX's spending. Musk has to show that he can turn losses into profits as he seeks earnings opportunities in the highly contested field. This is even as SpaceX's AI infrastructure will play a key role in Musk's aim to operate solar-powered data centers in the future.
  • SpaceX's Starship Push: Equally important, focus is on Starship, the megarocket SpaceX is developing as a reusable machine that can carry a human crew and other heavy payloads into orbit. According to CFRA analyst Keith Snyder, Starship reaching full commercial deployment is one of the several key investor bets behind SpaceX's lofty valuation. He believes that delays or failure in testing can quickly turn investors sour.
  • Starlink Growth: Not to be left out, Starlink, SpaceX's subsidiary focused on satellite communication services, is the company's current biggest profit earner. The unit has to continue delivering massive subscription growth and margin expansion to keep investors excited. Wolfe Research shares this sentiment.

Is SpaceX a Good Stock to Buy?

As today marks SpaceX's first trading day as a public company, analyst coverage remains limited. However, so far, the consensus rating is a Hold, suggesting mixed reactions.

This is based on the Sell decision by Snyder on June 12 and Buy rating by Oppenheimer's Timothy Horan the day before. Both ratings give an average price target of $152.50 that implies about 5% downside risk.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

S&P 500 (SPX)

Materials was the top-performing sector on Friday. Albemarle (ALB) and Mosaic Company (MOS) led the way, with both stocks up by over 7%. Furthermore, MOS closed higher after the European Union agreed to ease regulations across the fertilizer industry.

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the healthcare sector faced the largest drawdown, led by broad weakness across pharmaceutical and life sciences stocks. data.title: SpaceX Stock (SPCX) Rises 19% in Market Debut
data.description:
The stock of Elon Musk's SpaceX (SPCX) rose 19% in its market debut and finished its first trading day at $160.95 per share.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

The closing share price gives the company a market capitalization of $2.11 trillion, making it the eighth largest publicly traded company in the U.S. Shares had been given a set price of $135 as part of its initial public offering (IPO). The stock began trading at noon hour at $150 a share and climbed higher.

SpaceX successfully raised $75 billion from the IPO at an initial valuation of $1.77 trillion, making it the biggest share sale in history. Company founder Elon Musk, who is SpaceX's majority shareholder, has the distinction of becoming the first trillionaire in history with a net worth of $1.05 trillion. Musk also founded and runs electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA).

What's Next for SpaceX?

Analysts appear divided on SPCX stock and where the company goes from here. Many analysts have already issued ratings on the stock and they are a mix of Buy and Sell recommendations. Bearish analysts say that the company needs to justify its lofty valuation and point to the fact that SpaceX is unprofitable.

Bulls, on the other hand, see SpaceX as a leader in the commercial space sector and like the company's Starlink satellite internet business, Grok artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, and social media platform X. There are rumors that Musk might also bring Tesla under the SpaceX umbrella now that the IPO has been successfully completed.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy?

It's too early for a sizable number of analysts to have price targets on SpaceX stock. So let's look instead at Tesla, Musk's other publicly traded company. Tesla stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 30 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 12 Buy, 15 Hold, and three Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average TSLA price target of $404.54 implies less than 1% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
